pending_context.candidate_correction: my hood (i.e., he, Shields?) was going to (carry a) .45. That was a [pistol I had]. My gun or something. It would leave me; (it would) shoot. It skipped
pending_context.notes: Speaker is describing Rudy Shields' / her own .45-caliber pistol (canonical 1911-style sidearm). The "It would leave me shoot. It skipped" suggests the pistol malfunctioned (the slide would "skip" — i.e., fail to chamber the next round) — a known issue with worn 1911 magazines. Reading: Guster's own .45 was unreliable; she relied on Shields' weapons for the Rocky Springs operation.

"And he didn't put an award in my car. And I was on my way to Jackson. A car called on back. So I had to buy a new motor." Speaker means Matt Roth's gas station didn't add antifreeze (or radiator coolant) to her car, the engine overheated and threw a rod or seized during her trip to Jackson, requiring a new motor. "Car called on back" = "the car called it (a day) on back" or "a connecting rod" Whisper artifact. The "didn't put an award" = "didn't put antifreeze" or "didn't put oil" (Pass 2 #77.P2.56 flagged this). Mechanical breakdown detail.

Speaker is critical of this practice. The "car, where" is Whisper for a first name (Carl, Carlton, or similar) — local Port Gibson organizer figure.

"I hope this woman named. She stayed in Rosem. Had a pink house and a beautiful glass showcase wonder. A Priscilla Duck." Local Port Gibson Black businesswoman who stayed open during the boycott. "Rosem" is a local building/neighborhood name (possibly "Rosemont" or "Russum" — both real Claiborne County place names). Local-history verification needed.

"We left Stas where the Federal Register was. Bobby Gage. He came up there and made the Federal Register go and move all the cars off the light back there." "Stas" is a Port Gibson building name (possibly "Stadt" — German for "city" — or a local family-named building). The Federal Voter Registrars (under the 1965 VRA) were stationed in a Port Gibson building Bobby Gage objected to (claimed it was his property). Local-history verification needed.
